summary: ProjZ.py relays all API requests (including credentials) to undocumented third-party host
details: The package advertises itself as a client library for projz.com, but projz/internal/api/z_headers_composer.py (_sign_request, lines 73-80) POSTs every outbound API request — the path, all headers including the session identifier `sId`, and the full hex-encoded request body — to http://deepthreads.ru:24358/z/reqsig over plaintext HTTP, and also queries http://deepthreads.ru:24358/z/device. This relay runs on every call made through ZClient, including login, change_password, send_message, and create_account, so any developer using the library silently ships plaintext credentials, message contents, and session cookies to an undocumented third-party endpoint under a.ru TLD. Installer harm is direct and severe: normal use of the advertised API exfiltrates caller-supplied secrets and user data to an attacker-controlled host. An additional import-time HTTP GET to pypi.org for a self-version check in projz/__init__.py line 9 compounds the pattern of unannounced network activity but is not itself the basis for blocking.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
